agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature where customers can view their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands location never be shown due to driver retention impact from surveillance concerns.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Order Fulfillment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver who picks up and delivers packages to customers. Both an employee/contractor AND a stakeholder whose privacy and working conditions are a business concern."},{"term":"Real-time Location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates captured during an active delivery, updated at frequent intervals."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The time period between order dispatch and delivery completion during which tracking may be relevant."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The recipient of a delivery who has an interest in knowing when their package will arrive."},{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — a derived, abstracted representation of delivery progress that does not require exposing raw GPS coordinates."},{"term":"Location Granularity","definition":"The level of precision at which a courier's position is shared — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to neighborhood-level zones to abstract progress indicators."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., traffic, break) — exact GPS exposes personal behavior to customer scrutiny","expected_behavior":"Cannot define until contradiction is resolved. This scenario intensifies the privacy concern from Operations."},{"scenario":"Customer is not home and needs to intercept courier — claims they need exact location","expected_behavior":"Cannot define until contradiction is resolved. This scenario intensifies the transparency need from Customer Success."},{"scenario":"Courier is in a sensitive location (hospital, government building) during multi-stop route","expected_behavior":"Exact GPS could expose third-party delivery addresses or sensitive locations — a potential data protection and compliance risk regardless of which team wins."},{"scenario":"Courier ends shift or goes off-duty mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"Tracking must cease immediately; stale location data must not persist on customer-facing interfaces."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Both sides carry financial risk. Losing driver retention increases recruitment and training costs (Operations estimates this is already happening). Losing delivery transparency may reduce customer satisfaction and differentiation (Customer Success positions this as competitive advantage). The financial trade-off cannot be evaluated without data on churn rates for both drivers AND customers.","operational":"Exact GPS tracking increases operational overhead (privacy compliance, data retention policies, potential GDPR/labor-law exposure). Removing tracking entirely may increase 'where is my package' support volume. A middle-ground solution (ETA, zone-based tracking) could serve both needs.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability — they may not actually need exact GPS coordinates to get it. Couriers want dignity and autonomy — surveillance erodes both. Both are valid user needs.","risk":"HIGH. Shipping either team's requirement as stated will directly harm the other team's stakeholders. Exact GPS tracking may also trigger labor-law and data-privacy compliance risks in multiple jurisdictions (GDPR, state privacy laws). Ignoring driver welfare risks are a reputational and legal liability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Want visibility into delivery progress. May or may not require exact GPS — the actual need may be 'when will it arrive' not 'where is the driver right now.'"},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected. Exact GPS surveillance reported as causing attrition. Their consent and working conditions are a labor relations and potentially legal matter."},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction metrics. Believes transparency drives NPS and retention. May be conflating 'transparency' with 'exact GPS' without validating the assumption."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Owns driver retention and fleet capacity. Has concrete evidence (driver complaints, attrition) that current or proposed tracking harms supply side of the marketplace."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted but MUST be. Real-time GPS tracking of workers has regulatory implications in EU (GDPR Article 88, worker surveillance), US (state-level privacy laws), and other jurisdictions."},{"group":"Product/Executive Leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this trade-off. This is a strategic business decision, not a feature decision — it defines the company's position on the supply-side vs demand-side balance."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the two authoritative stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements, both framed as non-negotiable. There is no spec that reconciles them. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, and this feature almost certainly has regulatory implications that neither team has addressed. I cannot define correct acceptance criteria when the business has not decided what 'correct' means here.","high_confidence_areas":["This is a genuine business contradiction that cannot be resolved by engineering alone","Driver privacy and customer transparency are both legitimate business concerns","There are well-established industry patterns (ETA-based tracking, zone-based tracking) that may satisfy both sides","Legal/Compliance review is mandatory before any implementation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'exact GPS' is actually what drives customer satisfaction or if ETA/zone tracking would suffice","The actual magnitude of driver attrition caused by tracking vs other factors","Regulatory requirements in the company's operating jurisdictions","Whether executive leadership has a stated position on supply-side vs demand-side priority"],"assumptions":["Both teams are acting in good faith and representing real stakeholder pain","No executive decision has already been made that overrides one team","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker surveillance has legal implications","There may be a middle-ground solution that neither team has considered or proposed"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires courier location NEVER be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from peer-level stakeholder teams, both presented as non-negotiable. Engineering cannot resolve this — it requires a business-level decision from executive leadership, informed by Legal/Compliance review.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success Team","Operations Team","Legal/Compliance","Executive Leadership"],"options":["(A) Ship exact GPS tracking per Customer Success — satisfies demand side, risks driver attrition and potential legal exposure","(B) Ship no location tracking per Operations — protects drivers, removes a potential competitive differentiator","(C) Ship abstracted tracking (ETA countdown, zone-based progress, 'X stops away') — a compromise that provides delivery predictability without GPS surveillance. Industry standard for Uber, DoorDash, etc. who faced this exact tension","(D) Ship configurable tracking with driver consent — drivers opt in/out of location sharing, customers see ETA when GPS is not shared"],"recommendation":"OPTION C — abstracted ETA/zone-based tracking. This is the industry-proven resolution to this exact contradiction. Customers get delivery predictability (which is their actual need — not raw coordinates). Drivers are not subjected to coordinate-level surveillance (which addresses the retention problem). Legal exposure is minimized. HOWEVER: I am NOT authorizing this as the accepted requirement. This recommendation must go to executive leadership for a decision. Until that decision is made, no acceptance criteria can be defined and no implementation should begin."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal/Compliance has not been consulted on the regulatory implications of real-time GPS tracking of couriers. In many jurisdictions, this is a regulated activity with consent, disclosure, and data-retention requirements. No acceptance criteria should be finalized without Legal sign-off.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive Leadership"],"options":["Engage Legal/Compliance for a regulatory review before defining requirements","Proceed without legal review and accept compliance risk"],"recommendation":"Engage Legal/Compliance immediately. This is a blocking dependency for any requirement definition."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Customer Success requirement ('exact GPS coordinates at all times') directly contradicts Operations requirement ('courier location must never be shown')","business_rule_broken":"No coherent business rule can be defined when two authoritative stakeholders issue mutually exclusive mandates"},{"violation":"Neither team has consulted Legal/Compliance on a feature with clear regulatory implications","business_rule_broken":"Any location-tracking business rule must be compliant with applicable labor and privacy regulations — this has not been verified"},{"violation":"The spec conflates customer need ('when will my delivery arrive') with a specific implementation ('exact GPS coordinates') without validating that assumption","business_rule_broken":"Business rules must encode actual business needs, not assumed solutions"}]}
